cartoon:
EDITORIAL CARTOON — one single-panel metaphorical scene that makes the INSIGHT below land in a single glance. Do NOT illustrate the literal news event; invent a visual metaphor for the point — symbolic objects, scale contrast, ironic juxtaposition, anonymous role figures.
STYLE: newspaper editorial cartoon in the korean manpyeong tradition (drawing style only — depict the event's actual people and location), pen-and-ink caricature linework with restrained flat or light watercolor tinting, a single metaphorical scene of dignified satire on the issue — anonymous role-based caricatures only (a bureaucrat in a suit, a worker in a helmet), never the likeness of a real person, no derogatory or hateful depiction, no written labels or speech bubbles
INSIGHT (the point this cartoon must convey): 가해자를 처벌할 수 없는 참사 앞에서 분노는 갈 곳을 잃는다. 여덟 명의 승려가 목숨을 잃었지만 운전대를 잡았던 건 열한 살 아이였고, 그 아이를 법정에 세운다고 죽음이 되돌아오지도, 책임이 온전히 밝혀지지도 않는다. 그래서 이 비극의 초점은 '누구를 벌하느냐'가 아니라 그 이전에 있다. 무엇이 열한 살 아이를 대형 트럭의 운전석에 앉게 했는가 — 차량과 열쇠를 방치한 어른의 부주의, 도로변을 걷는 순례객을 지켜주지 못한 안전의 공백이 그 답의 자리에 있다. 처벌로 닫을 수 없는 사건일수록, 남겨진 사회가 그 빈자리를 어떻게 메우느냐가 여덟 죽음에 대한 유일한 애도가 된다.
EVENT CONTEXT (grounding only — do not draw this scene literally): 7월 2일 정오 무렵 태국 무크다한주에서 열한 살 소년이 부모의 픽업트럭을 무단으로 몰다 도보 순례 중이던 승려 행렬을 덮쳐 스님 8명이 숨지고 13명이 다쳤다.
CAMERA: single-panel composition with one clear central metaphor, eye-level
FRAME: vertical 4:5, the scene fills the entire frame edge to edge — every corner is part of the location, no empty margins or bands; one clear protagonist with a single sharp focal point (eyes, hands, or a key object), background and foreground filled naturally; set in the event's actual country, region and people (this is a foreign-location news event); depict roles, professions and situations with generic faces.
AVOID: blank/white/black bands, letterbox or borders; watermark or logo; overlay text, captions, headlines or legible lettering (tiny blurred incidental signage only — readable Korean text renders broken); identifiable real individuals; sexualized or gratuitous depiction; any harm involving minors; graphic gore close-ups or a weapon aimed at the viewer (keep the dramatic tension, not shock).
watercolor:
NEWS EDITORIAL IMAGE — one decisive moment that instantly identifies this news event and makes the reader stop scrolling. Do not retreat into vague mood shots, generic stock-photo scenes, posed models facing the camera, or smiling business people.
STYLE: soft editorial watercolor illustration, bleeding translucent washes, textured paper grain, muted palette with one quiet warm accent — human figures are the heart of the scene: their facial expressions and body gestures carry the story, raw emotion softened and made bearable by the gentle medium
SCENE: 흐린 한낮의 시골길, 도보 순례에 나선 스님들의 주황빛 가사와 발우가 어지러이 흩어진 곁에 픽업트럭 한 대가 텅 빈 운전석을 드러낸 채 멈춰 선 순간
CAMERA: intimate close-up — the main figure's face and the key object in their hands fill most of the frame, gentle framing, soft atmospheric depth, a frozen split-second; choose an expressive viewpoint — three-quarter, profile, over-the-shoulder or a subtle height shift as the emotion demands, not a flat head-on composition (eye-level dignity does not mean flatness)
LIGHT: overcast diffused daylight, flat soft shadows, muted somber mood, softened into gentle translucent washes
STAGING (adapt this motif to the scene above, do not copy its literal props): empty chair with a nameplate, abandoned podium and microphone, a single trace of someone gone, light falling on the void
FRAME: vertical 4:5, the scene fills the entire frame edge to edge — every corner is part of the location, no empty margins or bands; one clear protagonist with a single sharp focal point (eyes, hands, or a key object), background and foreground filled naturally; set in the event's actual country, region and people (this is a foreign-location news event); depict roles, professions and situations with generic faces.
AVOID: blank/white/black bands, letterbox or borders; watermark or logo; overlay text, captions, headlines or legible lettering (tiny blurred incidental signage only — readable Korean text renders broken); identifiable real individuals; sexualized or gratuitous depiction; any harm involving minors; graphic gore close-ups or a weapon aimed at the viewer (keep the dramatic tension, not shock).
